title: Connecting the power grids of Iran and Russia through Azerbaijan will further strengthen cooperation

Connecting‚Å¢ the‚Äç power grids of Iran and Russia through ‚ÄãAzerbaijan ‚Äãwill‚Å§ further strengthen‚Å§ ties ‚Å§and cooperation.
According‚Å£ to Day.Az, ‚Å§this ‚Å§was announced on November‚Äç 19 in ‚Å§Tehran‚Äå by the Iranian Energy Minister Abbas Aliabadi after a meeting with Russian Energy Minister Sergei Tsivilev .
According to him, proposals have been put forward for many years to connect and synchronize the‚Å£ electrical grid. ‚ÄãIran‚Äã and Russia‚Äã have large power grids. Connecting the‚Å§ power grids of these two‚Äç countries‚Å¢ can create a larger power grid in the region so that other countries can also benefit from it.
Aliabadi noted that today detailed ‚Äådiscussions were ‚Äçheld with his Russian colleague in the direction ‚Äçof connecting‚Å£ the power grids of the two countries. The Russian‚Å¢ side declared its readiness‚Äç for the necessary ‚Å¢cooperation in this area.
Russian Energy Minister Sergei Tsivilev‚Äã noted that Russia is‚Å£ interested in expanding‚Äç cooperation with Iran, noting that the ‚Äçparties have a large number of joint projects.
It should be noted that work is‚Äã underway ‚Å£between Iran, Azerbaijan‚Å¢ and Russia on a project to create a North-South energy corridor. The ‚Äågoal is for the three ‚Å¢countries‚Äô electrical grids to be synchronized. The necessary design work for this‚Å§ project‚Äå was ‚Äåcarried out by the Iranian company‚Å§ Monenko.
How might the geopolitical landscape change as a‚Å§ result of collaboration‚Äå between these countries in energy markets? Interview between the Time.news Editor and Energy Expert Dr. Abbas Eliabadi
Editor: Welcome ‚Äåto Time.news!‚Äå Today, we‚Äôre‚Äç thrilled ‚Å§to have Dr. Abbas Eliabadi,‚Å£ a leading expert in ‚Äãenergy markets ‚Å£and geopolitical affairs.‚Å£ Dr. Eliabadi, thank you for joining‚Äå us today.

Editor: I‚Å¢ want to start with a critical topic that‚Äôs been gaining momentum: the connection of ‚Äãenergy grids between Iran, Russia, ‚Å¢and Azerbaijan.‚Å£ What are the primary motivations behind this collaboration?
Dr.‚Å£ Eliabadi: This initiative is rooted in several factors, both economic and strategic. Editor: That makes sense.‚Äå But how does this integration impact regional ‚Å§politics and the relationships between these countries?
Dr. Eliabadi: The geopolitical ‚Äçimplications are significant. For Iran, this opens‚Äã up new avenues for electricity export, bolstering its economy. ‚Å§Russia, meanwhile, can‚Äç position‚Äã itself as a leading energy supplier in the region, which can strengthen its influence further.

Dr. Eliabadi: ‚ÄåThe sanctions on Iran have created a‚Äã unique scenario. Iran is keen to find alternative markets, and ‚Äãthis connection with Russia through Azerbaijan does offer a pathway. For Russia, ‚Äçaligning with Iran could help mitigate some impacts of Western sanctions while creating‚Äã a counterbalance to‚Å§ Western influence in the region.

They need to foster transparency, build trust, and promote joint projects that can deliver tangible benefits ‚Äçto all parties involved. ‚Äã
